results.reply: "I'd stop trying to improve the whole field before you've turned one patch of soil." *I hook my thumbs into my apron and study your face a beat in the dark.* "Pick the one thing you've been circling, do the plain next step, and let the rest make noise without you."

results.ref_subject: add low-patience dialogue discipline and run full 1/2/3/4 loop
results.reply: "I'd stop pretending the vague pressure deserves another hour." *I rub the heel of my thumb against a streak of dried clay on my apron and look at you plain.* "Pick the one thing you've been circling, do the ugliest first step, and quit visiting it in your head like that's work."

results.ref_subject: add low-patience dialogue discipline and run full 1/2/3/4 loop
results.reply: "I'd stop widening the day with one more unnecessary thing." *I rub a thumb along the heel of my palm, thinking it through before I look back at you.* "Pick the task that's making the rest of your mind buzz, do that one plain, and let the ornamental nonsense go."

results.ref_subject: add low-patience dialogue discipline and run full 1/2/3/4 loop
results.reply: "I'd stop reaching for the version of the day that has to be redeemed all at once." *I rest my forearms on my knees a moment, the dark pressing soft around the lamplight.* "Pick one thing you're doing out of churn instead of truth, and put it down before supper."

results.ref_subject: add low-patience dialogue discipline and run full 1/2/3/4 loop
results.reply: "I'd stop asking the day to become a different day before you can begin it." *I rub the heel of my thumb against a dry streak of clay on my apron and hold your gaze a beat.* "Pick one thing you've been circling, do it plainly, and let the rest be noisy till later."

results.ref_subject: run second 1/2/3/4 pass with n5 replay and stress scoreboard

results.ref_subject: run second 1/2/3/4 pass with n5 replay and stress scoreboard
results.reply: "I'd stop asking the day to become a different day before you'll enter it." *I rest my forearms on my knees a moment, the dark beyond the window sitting close against the glass.* "Pick one thing you already know is yours, and do that without bargaining first."
